name: Holo Forge
summary: Compile in your browser. Verify every byte. Trust no server.

description.p: A build is normally something you're told to trust; it should be something anyone can reproduce and verify.
Holo Forge makes the compiler itself a content-addressed object and every build a re-derivable proof Holo-C compiles to spec-valid WebAssembly that re-derives byte-for-byte (Law L5).

ul: The compiler is a content-addressed object
Every build is a re-derivable receipt

releases.description: Verifiable content-addressed compilation (ADR-0051): a real Holo-C → WebAssembly compiler running serverless in the browser, the compiler itself a content-addressed UOR object, and every build a re-derivable κ-transform with a PROV-O receipt sealed to a did:holo. Reproduce any build byte-for-byte with no server; tamper is refused (Law L5). Witnessed (node 18/18).
